_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA State Senate batch — Districts 6-10, all R (state tier). Pillion (SD6), Stanley (SD7), Peake (SD8), Brankley Mulchi (SD9), Cifers (SD10). Evidence-based finalization from primary roll-call records (LIS / VPAP / Richmond Sunlight), issue scorecards (ReproRising F-grades, Family Foundation of VA), and named crossover lists for the 2025/2026 constitutional amendments. All five HELD THE GOP LINE on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ3/SJ3) and the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247/HJR1) — none crossed over (the 2026 GOP SSM crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Suetterlein, +Jordan; none of these five). Records are thin-but-clean: every scored cell is a documented aligned vote/sponsorship/statement; dimensions with no public per-member evidence (election integrity individual lines, federal overreach, border, christian liberty for most) are left NULL, not inferred. Caucus-line-by-exclusion votes (e.g. AWB SB749, where the senator is in the documented NO bloc but the individual roll-call line was JS-blocked) were generally left null unless a HARD on-record statement or sponsored bill corroborated the cell.

profile.religion: Christian (generic faith reference in official bio; no denomination publicly named)
profile.background: VA State Senator, District 6 (far southwest VA — Buchanan, Dickenson, Lee, Russell, Scott, Washington, Wise + Bristol & Norton), sitting since Jan 2020 (seat renumbered from SD-40 in 2021 redistricting); former Delegate 2014-2020. Pediatric dentist from Abingdon; Major, VA Army National Guard (Iraq deployment); chairs the VA Opioid Abatement Authority. Term ends Jan 2028.

todd-pillion.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-6 (sitting since 2020; formerly SD-40). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Solid conservative HARD record with no documented deviations: pro-life (ReproRising 2025 grade F; voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment SJ247/HJR1); held the line on marriage (NOT among the 5 GOP crossovers who voted YES on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 on 01/16/2026 — i.e. he voted NO); fiscal conservative (Freedom Index 67%; voted against the SB1307 local-sales-tax option); pro-2A (voted NO on HB1174, which raised the handgun-purchase age and expanded the 'assault firearm' definition). Family/child-sovereignty, christian-liberty, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach, and border cells left NULL — no Pillion-specific public record found (caucus-line inferences on the felon-voting amendment and AWB SB749 were not converted to scores). Sourcing note: lis.virginia.gov/VPAP returned JS-shells/403 to automated fetch; named votes corroborated via WHRO, choicetracker.org, freedomindex.us, ReproRising PDF, Ballotpedia.

0.note: Consistent pro-life voting record; ReproRising 2025 grade F; voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 01/21/2025 and HJR1 01/31/2025) plus a string of NO votes on abortion-access bills.

Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, Senate 26-13 on 01/16/2026) — the only 5 GOP YES votes were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Suetterlein, Jordan; Pillion is not among them, so he voted to keep the one-man-one-woman constitutional provision.

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 NO).

2.note: Fiscal-restraint record (Freedom Index 67%); voted AGAINST SB1307, which would have authorized a 1% local sales-tax add-on; backed HB158 capping emergency executive orders at 45 days.

1.note: Voted NO on HB1174, which raised the handgun-purchase age to 21 and expanded the 'assault firearm' definition — opposed an expansion of assault-weapons/age restrictions.

profile.religion: Methodist (per secondary aggregator; not confirmed on a primary source)
profile.background: VA State Senator, District 7 (Franklin County / Rocky Mount / Moneta / Southside-to-Southwest VA), sitting since 2011 (seat renumbered from SD-20 in 2021 redistricting; originally SD-19). Attorney; former Franklin County GOP chair. Running for re-election (next election Nov 2027); term ends Jan 2028.

bill-stanley.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-7 (sitting since 2011; formerly SD-20/SD-19). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strong conservative HARD record, no deviations: pro-life (voted NO on SJ247 reproductive-freedom amendment 01/21/2025; ReproRising 2025 grade F); held the line on marriage (one of the 13 GOP NO votes on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 01/16/2026 — NOT a crossover, unlike Durant); SIGNATURE family-sovereignty win as CHIEF AUTHOR of SB1515 (2023), Virginia's online-pornography age-verification law signed by Youngkin; pro-law-enforcement sponsorships (SB86 fallen-officer service-handgun, SB100 volunteer-responder job protection); on-record state-sovereignty stance ('Repeal Amendment' / sovereignty over federal overreach) and state immigration-enforcement stance; strongly pro-2A (on record: 'the red flag bill is one of the worst bills I've ever seen,' opposed the assault-weapons ban). Election-integrity, christian-liberty specifics, and economic CBDC/ESG cells left NULL — no itemized record found. Sourcing note: lis.virginia.gov/VPAP/legiscan JS-blocked; votes corroborated via Cardinal News, Ballotpedia, ReproRising PDF, WTOP, Wikipedia.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, Senate 21-19 party-line 01/21/2025, all 19 R including Stanley opposed); ReproRising 2025 grade F.

Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, Senate 26-13 01/16/2026); the 5 GOP YES crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, Suetterlein — Stanley is one of the 13 GOP NO votes.

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 NO).

3.note: Chief patron/author of SB1515 (2023), Virginia's law requiring ID-based age verification for websites with >=33.3% pornographic content; signed by Gov. Youngkin, effective 07/01/2023.

0.note: Pro-law-enforcement sponsorships: SB86 (lets a fallen State Police officer's survivor purchase the service handgun for $1) and SB100 (protects volunteer emergency responders from retaliation); long tenure on Senate Courts of Justice.

3.note: On-record: supports the 'Repeal Amendment' and pledged to be 'a stalwart in preserving the sovereignty of the Commonwealth of Virginia over the over-reaching power of the federal government.'

2.note: On-record: 'I will ensure that our commonwealth will enforce the state law if the federal government will not' on immigration — supports state immigration enforcement over sanctuary policy.

1.note: On-record opposition to red-flag laws ('one of the worst bills I've ever seen come through this chamber') and to the assault-weapons ban; sponsored the pro-firearm SB86.

profile.religion: Methodist (listed on the official Virginia Senate member page)
profile.background: VA State Senator, District 8 (Bedford County, Campbell County, City of Lynchburg), sitting since 2017 (special election to SD-22; renumbered SD-8 in 2024 redistricting). Lynchburg attorney (30+ years); five children. Was elected RPV chairman April 2025 and resigned that party post (NOT the Senate seat) effective 12/31/2025. Term ends Jan 2028.

mark-peake.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-8 (sitting since 2017; formerly SD-22). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strong conservative HARD record, no deviations: pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247/HJR1, on-record parental-consent objection; ReproRising 2025 grade F; sponsored SB163 barring abortion-requiring surrogacy clauses); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment with explicit faith rationale — 'my faith teaches me marriage is between one man and one woman' — NOT a crossover, unlike Durant); strong parental-rights advocacy; CHIEF PATRON of voter-photo-ID and registration-verification bills (SB168, SB1070); pro-2A (on record: the assault-weapons ban is 'a direct infringement on the Second Amendment'). NOTE: he resigned the RPV CHAIRMANSHIP 12/31/2025 but remains the sitting SD-8 senator. Economic-stewardship, public-justice, federal-overreach, border, and christian-liberty cells left NULL — no itemized Peake-specific record found (VPAP donor page 403). Sourcing note: lis.virginia.gov JS-rendered; named votes corroborated via VPM, Virginia Mercury, Ballotpedia News, ReproRising PDF, Wikipedia, official Senate member page.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 01/21/2025; HJR1 01/16/2026), on-record opposing it over lack of parental consent / no age distinction; ReproRising 2025 grade F; sponsored SB163 to bar surrogacy contracts requiring abortion.

Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment, stating 'my faith teaches me marriage is between one man and one woman' and that he would 'campaign vigorously against' all three amendments; not among the GOP crossovers.

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (NOT among the 5 GOP YES votes: DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, Suetterlein).

Repeated, on-record parental-notification/consent advocacy — central to his objection that the abortion amendment 'doesn't distinguish between adults and minors so we're very concerned that will do away with parental consent.'
Chief patron of voter-photo-ID legislation (SB168, SB1070) and a registration-verification bill requiring registrars to match name/DOB/SSN against the SSA database.
On-record opposition to the 2026 assault-weapons ban (SB749), calling it 'a direct infringement on the Second Amendment'; Republicans (incl. Peake) in the NO bloc.

profile.religion: Baptist (Liberty University graduate; campaign cites faith as 'a top priority,' denomination not self-stated)
profile.background: VA State Senator, District 9 (Southside VA — Mecklenburg / Charlotte / Halifax region), in office since Jan 17, 2024 (won the January 2024 special election succeeding Frank Ruff). Realtor/residential contractor; ~20 years as legislative aide to Sen. Ruff and Del. Wright; former Clarksville Town Council member; founder of The Windsong Foundation (domestic-abuse victim support). Next full term on the ballot 2027.

tammy-brankley-mulchi.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-9 (sitting since Jan 2024 special election). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record (richest roll call of this batch, via Richmond Sunlight mirror of the official feed): pro-life (voted NO on SJ247/HJ1 2025 and HJ1/SJ1 2026 reproductive-freedom amendments, plus SB754, SB743; ReproRising 2025 grade F); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment in BOTH 2025 and 2026 — not among the GOP crossovers); affirms biological sex (chief patron of SB723/SB749 women's-sports bills and SJ49 'affirming biological differences between the sexes'); tough-on-crime victim/penalty sponsorships (SB673 stalking, SB700 repeat prostitution-solicitation penalties, SB616/SB653 protective orders, SB844 sex-offender registry) plus SB1420 (forfeited assets for law-enforcement use); strongly pro-2A (voted NO across the entire 2025-26 gun-control slate — assault-firearms ban SB1181, magazine limits, waiting period, mandatory storage — and YES on the SB1389 safety-device tax credit). Election-integrity left NULL: she cast many votes on Democratic-majority election bills but the per-bill direction could not be verified, so no integrity signal is scored (honest null). Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, border, family-choice, and economic CBDC/ESG cells left NULL. Sourcing note: VPAP/new LIS API/legiscan unreachable; roll calls from Richmond Sunlight (Open-Virginia mirror of the official Legislature feed), crossover list from Ballotpedia News.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1 2025; HJ1/SJ1 2026) and on SB754 and SB743; ReproRising 2025 grade F.
Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment in both 2025 (SJ249/HJ9) and 2026 (HJ3/SJ3); not among the GOP crossovers (DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Suetterlein).

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3/SJ3 NO, 2026).

2.note: Affirms biological sex: chief patron of SB723/SB749 (women's-sports participation by birth sex) and SJ49 ('rights of women; affirming biological differences between the sexes').

0.note: Pro-law-enforcement: voted YES on SB1420 (forfeited assets for law-enforcement use) and sponsored a suite of crime/public-safety bills (school security officers SB755).

2.note: Proportional punishment / victim protection: chief patron of SB673 (stalking), SB700 (increased penalties for repeat prostitution solicitation), SB616/SB653 (emergency protective orders), SB844 (sex-offender registry intervals).

1.note: Voted NO across the 2025-26 gun-control slate — assault-firearms & feeding-device ban (SB1181/HB1607), public-carry ban (SB880), 5-day waiting period (SB891), mandatory storage/locking (SB1134/SB1597); voted YES on SB1389 firearm-safety-device tax credit.

profile.background: VA State Senator, District 10 (central VA — Powhatan / Amelia / Cumberland / Goochland region), sworn in Jan 15, 2025 after winning the Jan 7, 2025 special election (~58-42 over Jack Trammell). Homeschooled; founder of YakAttack / Bonafide Kayaks / MITUSA Manufacturing (17 patents); father of five. Term ends Jan 2028 — record limited to the 2025-26 sessions.

luther-cifers.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-10 (sworn Jan 15, 2025 — special election Jan 7, 2025; CORRECTION to prior data that listed Nov 2023/Jan 2024). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Brand-new senator (~1.5 sessions) with a clean conservative record so far: Family Foundation of Virginia report card scored him 100% pro-family (covering abortion, same-sex marriage, school policy, surrogacy, physician-assisted suicide); pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247 with an on-record floor statement); held the marriage line (NOT among the GOP crossovers on the same-sex-marriage amendment in either 2025 or 2026); pro-2A (on-record opposition to the SB749 assault-weapons/magazine ban — 'flawed in every sense of the word... an attempt to take law-abiding Virginians and turn them into criminals'). Family-choice specifics, christian-liberty, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach, and border cells left NULL — given his short tenure, no per-bill record was found (the Family Foundation 100% is a diffuse aggregate, used only to corroborate the life/marriage cells). Sourcing note: VPAP/LIS/legiscan blocked; votes corroborated via infocville, vadogwood, gomag, WHRO, 29news, Family Foundation report card, Ballotpedia.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, 01/21/2025) with an on-record floor statement opposing it; Family Foundation of VA scored him 100% pro-family (abortion votes included).

Did NOT cross over on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — in 2025 only 3 Republicans (DeSteph, Diggs, Durant) voted YES and in 2026 only the named crossovers did; Cifers was in the GOP NO bloc both times.

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage; Family Foundation of VA counted his same-sex-marriage votes pro-family (100% record).
On-record opposition to the 2026 assault-weapons/10-round-magazine ban (SB749): 'flawed in every sense of the word... an attempt to take law-abiding Virginians and turn them into criminals.'
